title: Why Positive Reinforcement Dog Training Works: Science-Backed Benefits and Methods
raw_text: Science-backed article covering positive reinforcement (R+) for dog training. Cites University of Lincoln study in Frontiers in Veterinary Science comparing R+ vs electronic collars. Dogs trained with rewards responded faster, higher first-command compliance, stronger obedience. American Kennel Club expert advice cited. Operant conditioning with immediate rewards (treats, toys, play), clicker training for precise behavior marking, reward-based obedience without fear or coercion. Short 5-15min sessions recommended. Tailored reward systems per dog's preferences.

metadata.notes: Professional SF training provider, cites peer-reviewed studies and AKC
